steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Find the total perimeter of the squares
steps.content: Each square has 4 sides of length $r$, so each square has perimeter $4r$. With 3 squares: $$3 \times 4r = 12r$$

steps.title: Find the total perimeter of the triangles
steps.content: Each equilateral triangle has 3 sides of length $t$, so each triangle has perimeter $3t$. With 6 triangles: $$6 \times 3t = 18t$$

steps.title: Write the total and eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
The combined perimeter is $12r + 18t = 270$.
  • A: $9r + 24t = 270$ — Wrong coefficients; 3 squares give $12r$, not $9r$. Eliminated.
  • B: $3r + 6t = 270$ — Uses counts instead of perimeters (ignores side counts). Eliminated.
  • C: $12r + 18t = 270$ — $3 \times 4r = 12r$ and $6 \times 3t = 18t$. That is the correct answer.
  • D: $6r + 3t = 270$ — Swaps the shape counts and ignores side counts. Eliminated.
